title: hello.food CLI scope-tier + enforcement contract v1
description: Single canonical machine-readable contract for the least-privilege scope model shared between the private backend (frntrllc/hellofood) and language-neutral clients (the Rust heyfood rewrite; released Python CLI v0.3.2 until cutover). SOURCE OF TRUTH for: the CLI scope inventory (the server ceiling), the TIER semantics (required-envelope vs optional, and how they compose with the orthogonal server-ahead rollout axis), the canonical scope-order law, and the TYPED scope-denial error shapes at the tool layer and the REST layer. The backend parity test (tests/test_scope_tier_contract.py) verifies the cli_scopes tier tuples, the enforcement predicate, and the recorded wire fixtures against THIS file; nothing is hand-duplicated. The three-layer parity (ceiling <-> CHANNEL_OAUTH_ALLOWED_SCOPES <-> auth UI SCOPE_LABELS) is additionally pinned by tests/test_cli_authorization_contract.py.

scope_inventory.description: The complete server ceiling: every scope the token endpoint will accept and the consent page labels. Canonical order is significant (see scope_order_law). The authoritative Python tuple is app.core.cli_scopes.CLI_SESSION_SCOPES.

tiers.description: TIER is an AUTHORITY/GATING axis: does holding a scope gate POST /v1/agent/converse ADMISSION, or does it merely unlock an additional tool/endpoint when granted? Every scope is in exactly one tier. Orthogonal to the SERVER-AHEAD rollout axis (see server_ahead).

required_envelope.description: The complete agent tool envelope. A CLI session missing ANY of these is 403'd at converse admission. FROZEN at its historical value so the tier refactor invalidates zero existing sessions.

optional.description: Scopes that, when granted, expose an additional agent tool (filtered from the model's tool list otherwise) and/or a REST endpoint, but NEVER gate converse admission. A session without an optional scope converses exactly as before. EMPTY today: no deployed tool declares an optional scope. Grocery Phase A appends grocery:read / grocery:write here as a pure addition.

excluded_from_envelope.description: Ceiling scopes that are NOT part of the agent tool envelope AND are not optional agent-tool scopes: they gate their own dedicated surfaces (login/teardown, client-side transcription, dedicated menu-watch / health-integration endpoints), not converse and not an agent tool. Listed so the required-envelope derivation is exhaustive: required_envelope = ceiling MINUS excluded_from_envelope MINUS optional.

server_ahead.description: SERVER-AHEAD is a ROLLOUT/SEQUENCING axis, ORTHOGONAL to tier. It records scopes the server ceiling already declares but the released public CLI has not yet added to its LOGIN_SCOPES (the token endpoint rejects unknown scopes, so the server must lead — the v0.3.0 incident class). A scope can be server-ahead in EITHER tier. Pinned by tests/test_cli_authorization_contract.py::SERVER_AHEAD_SCOPES. Grocery Phase A introduces grocery:* as OPTIONAL-tier AND server-ahead simultaneously: never gates converse (tier) and is added to the CLI LOGIN_SCOPES only after the server ships it (sequencing). Wiring the optional tier does not touch this machinery.
server_ahead.orthogonality_rule: tier(scope) and server_ahead(scope) are independent; any optional scope MUST also be a member of scope_inventory.scopes (the ceiling) before it can be granted.

scope_order_law.description: Canonical scope order is server-pinned. Documented in docs/plans/2026-07-19-heyfood-cli-rust-transition-handoff.md section 2. The server pins order at token time; consented_cli_session_scopes returns the grant in CLI_SESSION_SCOPES order. NEW scopes APPEND LAST — never inserted mid-list — so a persisted grant's order is stable across releases.
rules: The order of scope_inventory.scopes is authoritative and equals app.core.cli_scopes.CLI_SESSION_SCOPES.
A newly introduced scope (e.g. grocery:read, grocery:write) is appended after the current last element, never inserted earlier.
consented_cli_session_scopes(grant) returns the intersection with the ceiling in this canonical order, regardless of the order the client requested.

scope_denial_errors.description: Two enforcement layers deny an unauthorized scope with DISTINCT, typed shapes. Neither ever produces a 500.

tool_layer.description: The conversation tool registry re-checks a tool's required_scope at execution (defense in depth; the tool is also filtered from the model's tool list up front). Denial is a TYPED tool result — a JSON object returned to the agent loop, never an exception. Enforcement is active only when the caller threaded a scope grant into ToolContext.metadata['granted_scopes'].

tool.description: The tool name that was refused.

rest_layer.description: The RequireScope FastAPI dependency (app.core.scope_enforcement.RequireScope) gates a REST endpoint on a single scope, sharing the tool-layer predicate (scope_grant_satisfies). Denial is HTTP 403 with the standard structured error envelope (code=forbidden, details.reason=scope_not_permitted), byte-identical to enforce_scoped_token's shape. Unrestricted *:full_access principals bypass the check; a scoped principal (e.g. a CLI session) missing the scope is denied.

enforcement_predicate.description: One shared predicate governs both layers so they admit/deny identically. app.core.cli_scopes.scope_grant_satisfies(required_scope, granted_scopes).
rules: required_scope is None -> allowed (tool/endpoint declares no scope).
granted_scopes is None -> allowed (unrestricted / non-scoped caller context; no filtering).
the grant contains any *:full_access scope -> allowed (phone/app/legacy principals bypass least-privilege gating).
